summary.intent: Implement a Nostr DM channel for the Moltis messaging platform, adding full parity with existing channels (Telegram, Discord, Slack, etc.) including inbound/outbound DMs, access control, OTP pairing, web UI, docs, metrics, and integration tests.
summary.outcome: Successfully shipped the complete Nostr channel across 6 commits: a new `crates/nostr/` Rust crate (~1700 LOC), web UI add/edit/onboarding flows, Playwright E2E tests, documentation, metrics instrumentation, NIP-44 fallback decryption, 5 integration tests with real relay round-trips, and PR review fixes addressing config sharing, security, and async correctness issues.

repo: ChannelType variants require updates across: as_str(), display_name(), secret_fields(), classify_chat(), descriptor(), FromStr, ALL constant, and the descriptor() match — ChannelType is #[non_exhaustive] so exhaustive matches elsewhere use wildcard arms and don't need updating.

Channel configs flow through ChannelsConfig via either named fields (telegram, slack, etc.) or serde flatten extra map — matrix was going through extra before this PR and should stay that way to avoid unintended behavioral changes.
channels.offered default controls which channels appear in the web UI picker — new channels must be added to this default Vec in schema.rs.
The contract test suite (crates/channels/src/contract.rs) is designed for TestPlugin, not real channel plugins that require valid credentials; real plugins need their own integration tests.
The onboarding flow (onboarding-view.js) has a separate ChannelTypeSelector and ChannelStep form routing that must be updated independently from the main page-channels.js add/edit modals.
metrics feature in gateway Cargo.toml uses dep?/feature syntax (e.g. moltis-nostr?/metrics) to avoid accidentally pulling in optional deps when only metrics is enabled without nostr.
The ALL constant test in crates/channels/src/plugin.rs checks variant count — must be updated when adding new ChannelType variants.

code.finding: nostr-sdk notifications() returns a broadcast::Receiver that must be created BEFORE connect()/subscribe() or events sent during connection setup will be missed — receiver must be obtained early and held.

nostr-sdk prelude imports nip44 with {self, *} making both nip44::decrypt and decrypt directly in scope — causes ambiguity with nip04::decrypt; use nostr_sdk::nips::nip44::decrypt fully qualified to avoid the conflict.

PR review: config was cloned into AccountState at start_account time — bus operated on a stale copy. Fix: share Arc<RwLock<NostrAccountConfig>> between state and bus so runtime config updates (allowlist changes) propagate without restart.
PR review: update_account_config was vulnerable to replacing a live nsec with a redacted placeholder — need a merge guard that rejects writes where secret_key == REDACTED_PLACEHOLDER.
PR review: allowlist pubkeys were re-parsed from strings on every inbound DM — fix by caching Vec<PublicKey> in AccountState alongside the shared config Arc.

workflow: nostr-sdk v0.44 has nip04 and nip44 as non-default features — must explicitly enable them in workspace Cargo.toml features list or encrypt/decrypt functions won't compile.
nostr-sdk Filter::pubkey() filters on #p tag (recipient), not event author — correct method for matching DMs addressed to the bot.
nostr-sdk Client::subscribe() takes a single Filter not Vec<Filter>; Client::disconnect() returns () not Result.
PublicKey::to_bech32() and SecretKey::to_bech32() return Result<String, Infallible> — need .expect() or .unwrap() before using as String, not direct string coercion.
Biome linting must be run on JS files after changes (from the crates/web/ui directory); taplo fmt formats TOML files alphabetically and will reorder keys in unrelated crates.
Integration tests using broadcast::Receiver for relay events must obtain the receiver before any async operations that might trigger events, otherwise messages are missed and tests time out.

friction: nostr-sdk nip04/nip44 features not enabled by default caused initial compilation failures — not obvious from the crate docs.
nostr-sdk prelude glob import causes name collision between nip04::decrypt and nip44::decrypt, requiring fully qualified paths despite both being legitimately in scope.
DM round-trip integration test timed out initially because broadcast::Receiver was created after connect() — required restructuring the test to obtain receiver first.
taplo fmt reordered keys in unrelated Cargo.toml files (msteams, secret-store, webhooks) creating noise in the diff.

Adding matrix as a named field in ChannelsConfig (unintentional side effect) required a follow-up revert commit to avoid behavioral changes for existing matrix configs going through serde flatten extra.
open_items: NIP-17 gift-wrapped DMs — more complex event handling, less widely supported, deferred to future.
NIP-65 relay list advertisement — future enhancement.
Seen tracker persistence to data_dir()/nostr_seen.json with debounced writes — the seen.rs LRU is in-memory only; persistence across restarts was not implemented.

GitHub Actions CI workflow update — secrets (NOSTR_TEST_BOT_KEY, NOSTR_TEST_SENDER_KEY) need to be added and a provider-integration style workflow job needs to be created to run #[ignore] integration tests.
Metrics have no counters/histograms actually recorded yet for relay health (relay connect/disconnect events) — only inbound/outbound message counts and active account gauge are instrumented.
